questions.description: The live queue currently has issues #15 and #36-#42.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Resolve all implementation-ready issues (#36-#41), resolve or re-triage human-decision issues (#15 and #42), and close each issue only after validation and a merged/pushed fix or explicit maintainer decision.

questions.prompt: How should blocked or human-decision issues be handled?
questions.description: Issue #15 is blocked/ready-for-human. Issue #42 asks for a trusted-input strategy decision before implementation.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Do the research, write the recommendation, update the issue with the proposed decision, and proceed only when the implementation path is clear. Do not invent a native-input architecture without evidence.

questions.prompt: What execution order should the plan optimize for?
questions.description: The Electron issues have natural dependencies: launch/attach and main inspector bugs unblock richer testing; renderer assertions/network/perf build on target dispatch.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Fix foundational launch/attach and inspector correctness first (#36, #37, #38), then renderer command parity (#39-#41), then trusted input (#42), then revisit #15 against the clarified product direction.

questions.prompt: What is the done condition for this goal?
questions.description: This controls whether the goal stops at local fixes, pushed commits, PRs, or closed issues.
questions.recommendedAnswer: All open issues are either closed with validated fixes/decisions pushed to GitHub, or explicitly documented as still blocked with the current blocker and next human action.
